name: Zorya
description: Zorya are two goddesses in Slavic mythology, representing the morning and evening stars. They are often referred to collectively as the Auroras. Zorya Utrennyaya, the Morning Star, opens the gates of the sun god Dazhbog's palace each morning so he can begin his daily journey across the sky. Zorya Vechernyaya, the Evening Star, closes the gates after him, ensuring his safe return for the night. They are described as radiant maidens, sometimes depicted as sisters or even as the same goddess appearing at different times. Their role is crucial in the cosmic order, ensuring the cyclical nature of day and night. They are also often associated with protection, particularly of warriors. Prayers and invocations were offered to them for strength and safety in battle. Their connection to the sun god further solidifies their importance within the Slavic pantheon, highlighting their integral role in the daily life and spiritual beliefs of the ancient Slavs.
subtitle: The Auroras, Morning and Evening Stars

keyMyths.title: The Opening and Closing of the Gates
keyMyths.description: Each morning, Zorya Utrennyaya awakens and opens the gates of Dazhbog's solar palace, allowing him to begin his journey across the sky, bringing light and warmth to the world. This act is essential for the continuation of life. In the evening, Zorya Vechernyaya closes the gates, ensuring Dazhbog's safe return to his palace for the night, protecting him from the darkness and unseen dangers. This cyclical duty highlights their importance in maintaining cosmic balance.

keyMyths.title: Guardians of the Doomsday Hound
keyMyths.description: According to some legends, the Zorya are tasked with guarding the doomsday hound, Simargl, who is chained to the constellation Ursa Minor. If Simargl ever breaks free from his chains, he will devour the sun, bringing about the end of the world. The Zorya must forever keep watch, ensuring the hound remains bound and preventing the cataclysmic event from occurring. Their vigilance is paramount to the survival of existence itself.
keyMyths.source: Afanasyev, Alexander. *Poetic Outlook on Nature by the Slavs*.

keyMyths.title: Protection of Warriors
keyMyths.description: Warriors often invoked the Zorya, particularly Zorya Utrennyaya, before battle, seeking her protection and strength. It was believed that her blessings would grant them courage and ensure their safety on the battlefield. Their association with light and the dawn symbolized hope and victory, making them powerful figures for those facing the dangers of war. Prayers were offered to them for guidance and to ward off evil influences.
